title: westia bifoliolata
intro: Westia bifoliolata, also known as West Indian quinine, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties, making it a potential treatment for fever and other illnesses caused by parasites. The health benefits of Westia bifoliolata include reducing inflammation, fighting off malaria, and alleviating symptoms of flu and other viral infections. Westia bifoliolata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
